icon.errBadType: Only PNG, JPEG, or SVG images are allowed.
icon.errTooLarge: Image must be smaller than 256 KB.

channels.title: Channels
channels.lead: One channel pairs a base URL with credentials and a wire format. Every model you publish later hangs off one of these channels.

fields.name: Name
fields.apiFormatHint: Only affects chat models. Embedding and image models ignore this setting.
fields.baseUrlHint: Leave blank to use the vendor default.
fields.apiKeyHintEdit: Leave blank to keep the saved key.

channels.editorDescriptionNew: Each channel routes one provider format (OpenAI/Claude/Gemini) to a base URL.
channels.editorDescriptionEdit: Update the channel. Leave the API key blank to keep the existing secret.
channels.removeBody: Removes "{{name}}" and cascades to every model that uses it.

models.title: Models
models.lead: The catalogue users see in the model picker. Chat, image and embedding kinds all live here; the channel's type determines which provider format runs upstream.
models.empty: No models yet — create a channel first, then add models that hang off it.
models.newDialogLead: Just the essentials to register the model — fine-tune behaviour and pricing afterwards from the model's settings page.

fields.label: Display label
fields.officialToolsHint: OpenAI runs its built-in tools (web search / code interpreter / image generation); the system's self-built tools are disabled.
fields.sortOrderHint: Lower numbers appear first. Use the arrows on the model list for quick reordering.
fields.moderationHint: When on, each user prompt is screened (no history). Configure the keyword list / moderation model on the Moderation page.

models.removeBody: Removes "{{label}}". Existing conversations keep working but cannot send new turns.

errors.missingFields: Channel, label and request_id are required

models.permissionsLead: Choose which user groups may use this model, and how much each gets per period. Leave every group off to keep the model open to everyone.
models.tagsHint: Assign tags to group this model under the picker’s filter chips.

skills.title: Skills
skills.lead: Reusable how-to documents the assistant pulls into context on demand. Each skill is one entry in the registry — the model decides whether to load it.

fields.name: Name
fields.whenHint: This goes into the system index — write it as a trigger condition for the model.
fields.importMdHint: Paste an official-format SKILL.md (frontmatter with name / description) to auto-fill the fields below.

skills.importFailed: Couldn't parse — check the SKILL.md format (needs --- frontmatter ---).

errors.missingFields: Name, description and instructions are required.

oauth.title: Login methods
oauth.lead: Social / OAuth sign-in. Add Google, GitHub or Apple, or any OAuth2/OIDC provider with a custom name and icon. Configured methods appear on the login screen automatically; with none set up, the login screen shows email + password only.
oauth.empty: No login methods yet — users sign in with email + password.

fields.name: Button label
fields.clientSecretHintEdit: Leave blank to keep the saved secret.
fields.scopesHint: Space-separated. Leave blank for the default (openid email profile).
fields.redirectUriHint: Register this exact URL in the provider's console as the allowed callback / redirect URI.
fields.redirectUriNew: Save the method first — the redirect URI to register with the provider appears once it has an ID.

hints.google: Create an OAuth client in Google Cloud → APIs & Services → Credentials, then paste the Client ID and Client secret.
hints.github: Create a GitHub OAuth App (Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps), then paste the Client ID and Client secret.
hints.apple: Create a Services ID and an AuthKey (.p8) in the Apple Developer portal. The Client ID is the Services ID; paste the .p8 contents as the private key and fill the Team ID and Key ID.
hints.oidc: Any OAuth2 / OpenID Connect provider. Supply the client credentials and the authorize / token / userinfo endpoints.

oauth.removeBody: Removes "{{name}}". Accounts created through it keep working and can sign in with email + password after a reset.

users.title: Users
users.lead: Create accounts, reset passwords, change roles, and suspend abusive accounts (revokes outstanding sessions immediately).
users.newLead: Create an account directly — no email verification. They can sign in immediately.
users.selfRoleHint: You can't change your own role — use another admin account.

fields.name: Name
fields.passwordEditHint: Leave blank to keep the current password.
fields.permanentCreditsHint: Non-expiring credit balance. Spent after the user's timed credits run out.
fields.groupHint: Determines which models this member can use and their quotas.

errors.passwordShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.

users.deleteBody: Permanently deletes "{{title}}" and all its messages. This cannot be undone.
users.conversationsLead: Triage view of every conversation owned by this user — for support and abuse. You can delete a conversation for cleanup; no edit, regenerate, or fork from here.
users.twofaHint: Reset if the member lost their authenticator device.

usage.title: Usage
usage.lead: Live aggregate of usage_logs — every model call (chat, task, image, embedding) is captured and priced from the model record.
usage.empty: No calls in the selected window.

documents.title: Documents
documents.lead: Everything that turns an upload into searchable context: which embedding model owns the KB vectors, where MinerU parses non-text files, where the bucket lives, and what extensions are allowed in.
documents.embeddingLead: The model used to vectorise KB documents. Changing this only affects newly-ingested chunks — existing vectors stay on the previous dimension. Leave blank to fall back to the EMBEDDING_* env defaults.
documents.embeddingModelHint: Picks from models with kind=embedding. Add one in /admin/models if the dropdown is empty.

tools.title: Tools
tools.lead: Outbound services the assistant calls during a conversation. Configure once here, live-reloaded for every subsequent tool call — no restart.

audio.title: Voice
audio.lead: Speech-to-text for the composer's microphone. Audio is sent to an OpenAI-compatible /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint with the model below. Live-reloaded — no restart.

fields.modelHint: e.g. whisper-1. Sent as the `model` field to the transcription API.
fields.baseUrlHint: Defaults to https://api.openai.com. Point at any OpenAI-compatible audio endpoint.
fields.apiKeyHint: Bearer token for the transcription endpoint. Stored plaintext. Leave blank to disable voice input.

settings.title: Global settings
settings.lead: The few knobs that affect everyone: default chat model, internal task model, long-context compression, signups, daily quotas.

fields.taskModelHint: Used for titles, query routing, compression summaries, memory triage.
fields.tokenTriggerHint: Compact older turns once the whole prompt (system + tools + history) exceeds this many tokens. 0 disables the token trigger.
fields.registerCaptchaHint: New sign-ups must solve a slider puzzle (drag the piece into the gap — no text to read).
fields.registerIpDailyLimitHint: Max accounts one IP address can create per day. 0 = unlimited.
fields.emailVerificationHint: New registrations must verify their email with a 6-digit code before signing in.
fields.domainWhitelistHint: Comma-separated. Only these domains can register. Leave empty to allow all.
fields.smtpLead: Outgoing mail for verification codes and password resets. Changes take effect on the next send — no restart required.
fields.smtpPasswordHint: App password or API key. Stored in plaintext.
fields.smtpFromHint: Sender shown in the inbox. Defaults to the username.
fields.smtpTlsHint: Enable for port 465. Leave off for 587 STARTTLS.
fields.sandboxUrlHint: Leave blank to use the bundled sandbox (provided via SANDBOX_BASE_URL). Only fill this to point at an external sandbox. If it's unset everywhere, python_execute stays in safe mode (no real execution).
fields.sandboxKeyHint: Bearer key sent with sandbox calls; stored in plaintext. Leave blank to use the bundled key (SANDBOX_API_KEY).
fields.storageLead: Where the sandbox archives /workspace when a session is reaped. Pick exactly one provider; leave blank to disable archive (reaped sessions are gone).
fields.storageProviderHint: S3-compatible (AWS, MinIO, etc.) or Aliyun OSS.
fields.storagePrefixHint: Object key prefix for archived workspaces, e.g. workspaces/.
fields.s3EndpointHint: Optional. Required for MinIO / non-AWS endpoints.
fields.mineruLead: All non-text uploads (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, images) flow through MinerU. The server first stores the file in the bucket above, then hands MinerU a presigned URL — so credentials never leave the operator's infrastructure. Pipeline model with OCR on.
fields.mineruBaseUrlHint: Cloud default: https://mineru.net. Self-hosted: point at your gateway. The Aurelia server hits /api/v4/extract/task under this URL.
fields.mineruTokenHint: Bearer token created in the MinerU console. Stored plaintext, like other channel keys.
fields.searchLead: Backend for web_search. Self-host SearXNG for a no-key option that stays inside your network; Serper / Brave are paid SaaS. Live-reloaded — changes apply on the next web_search call without restart.
fields.searchProviderHint: SearXNG needs only a base URL. Serper / Brave need an API key.
fields.searchBaseUrlHint: Root of your SearXNG instance, e.g. https://searx.your-domain.tld — Aurelia appends /search?q=…&format=json.
fields.uploadsLead: Extension allowlist enforced on every upload route. Leave blank to use the safe default (PDF / Office / images / common text). The check looks at the LAST extension, so report.pdf.exe is treated as exe.
fields.uploadAllowedExtHint: Comma-separated, case-insensitive, with or without dots — e.g. "pdf, docx, txt, png".

groups.title: User Groups
groups.lead: Membership tiers. Each group sets a price and feature list; per-model usage caps are configured on the model editor.
groups.creditsRatioHint: Shared by every group: 1 USD of model cost = this many credits. 0 disables credits platform-wide.
groups.quotaMsgHint: Shown when a member reaches a model’s usage limit for their group.
groups.quotaMsgPlaceholder: You’ve reached your plan’s limit for this model. Upgrade your plan to continue.
groups.editorLead: Name, price and features are shown to members on the subscription page.
groups.removeBody: Members of “{{name}}” will be moved to the free group. This cannot be undone.

fields.name: Name
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: For power users who need more headroom
fields.researchHint: Allow this group to use the Deep Research mode.
fields.featuresHint: One per line. Shown as a checklist on the subscription page.
fields.featuresPlaceholder:
Priority access
Higher usage limits
Early features
fields.buyUrlHint: Optional. Shown as a “Buy / Upgrade” button on the subscription page.
fields.creditsLead: Per-cycle timed allowance and refresh period for this group. The USD→credit rate is global (set below the group list).
fields.creditsPerUsdHint: 1 USD of model cost = this many credits. 0 disables credits for the group.
fields.creditAllowanceHint: Timed credits granted each refresh; unused are voided.
fields.creditPeriodHint: How often timed credits reset to the allowance.

quota.restrictedHint: Only the groups enabled below can use this model. Everyone else sees it locked.
quota.openHint: No groups enabled — this model is open to everyone, unlimited.
quota.unlimitedHint: Set to 0 for unlimited within the window.
quota.capHint: Cap per window for this group.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.lead: Usage trends across the platform — overall, by model, and by user.
analytics.empty: No usage in this period yet.

announcement.title: Announcement
announcement.lead: Show a global notice to users when they open the app. Add an image to make it an image announcement (image left, text right).
announcement.enabledHint: When off, no notice is shown.
announcement.rememberHint: When off, the notice shows on every visit even after the user closes it.
announcement.imageHint: Paste an image URL or upload one (PNG/JPG, ≤256 KB). With an image it becomes an image announcement.
announcement.bodyHint:
Supports HTML (links, bold, lists, headings…). Line breaks need
or

.
announcement.bodyPlaceholder: What do you want users to know?

moderation.title: Moderation
moderation.lead: Screen each user prompt before generation. Per-model on/off and mode are set on each model’s edit page.
moderation.keywordsHint: One keyword per line. In keyword mode, a prompt containing any of these is blocked. Leave empty to disable keyword screening.
moderation.modelHint: Used by models set to “model” mode — the prompt is sent here for an ALLOW/BLOCK verdict before generation.
moderation.categoriesHint: One per line. In “model” mode the moderation model screens the prompt against these categories (e.g. politics, pornography, violence).
moderation.messageHint: Shown to the user when a prompt is blocked.

redeemCodes.title: Redeem codes
redeemCodes.lead: Generate codes that grant a user group for a fixed duration. Users redeem them from the subscription page; each code is single-use by default.
redeemCodes.empty: No codes yet — generate a batch to hand out.

fields.batchNameHint: Internal label so you can find this batch later. Not shown to redeemers.
fields.quantityHint: How many codes to generate in this batch (1–1000).
fields.groupHint: The user group a redeemer is moved to.
fields.durationDaysHint: How long the membership lasts after redemption. Leave 0 for permanent.
fields.maxUsesHint: Defaults to 1 (single use). Increase for shared promo codes.
fields.expiresAtHint: After this date unredeemed codes can no longer be used. Leave blank for no expiry.
fields.notePlaceholder: Anything you want to remember about this batch

redeemCodes.newLead: Each generated code can be redeemed once and grants the configured group + duration.
redeemCodes.revokeBody: Disables "{{code}}". An unredeemed code becomes unusable; already-redeemed memberships keep working.
redeemCodes.removeBody: Permanently deletes "{{code}}" and its redemption record. Memberships already granted by this code keep working until they expire on their own. This cannot be undone.
redeemCodes.batchRemoveBody: Permanently deletes all unused and redeemed codes in this batch.

sandbox.title: Sandbox files
sandbox.none: No sandbox session for this conversation.

backup.title: Backup & Migration
backup.lead: Export the full database as a portable archive, or restore one to migrate this deployment to another server — or between SQLite and PostgreSQL.

export.title: Export backup
export.lead: Downloads every table as an engine-neutral archive you can restore on any Aurelia instance.
export.includeFilesHint: Bundles the uploads and artifacts directories for a complete, one-click migration. Larger archive.

import.title: Import & restore
import.lead: Upload an archive exported above to load it into this deployment.
import.warning: This replaces ALL existing data — every user, conversation, model and setting is wiped and overwritten by the archive. This cannot be undone. Back up first.
import.successSummary: Restored {{rows}} rows across all tables and {{files}} files.
import.reloginNote: Your session has ended — signing you out. Sign in again with an account from the imported data.

confirm.title: Replace all data?
confirm.lead: This permanently overwrites every record in this deployment with the uploaded archive.

modelTags.title: Model Tags
modelTags.lead: Create labels you can assign to models. Users filter the model picker by them.
modelTags.empty: No tags yet. Add one above.
modelTags.emptyHint: No tags yet — create them on the Model Tags page.
